Title: An alternate route for cellulose microfibril biosynthesis in plants

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase A (CESA) family; glycosyltransferase with transmembrane helices, RING domain, plant-conserved region, class-specific region, catalytic D and QxxRW motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary/secondary wall cellulose microfibril synthesis via rosette cellulose synthesis complexes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains that assemble into cellulose microfibrils at the plasma membrane
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides structural support and cell wall integrity during plant growth and development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall of protonemal filaments and gametophores
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PpCESA5 expression is sufficient to support normal protonemal development and partially rescues gametophore development in otherwise CESA-deficient moss; complete loss of CESA activity abolishes gametophore development but not protonemal cellulose microfibril formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; constitutive complementation/expression under rice Actin1 promoter; PCR and sequencing verification; microscopy/time-lapse imaging; cellulose structural analyses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported to be sufficient for normal development of protonemal filaments and leafy gametophores
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard moss culture conditions; protonemal tip growth versus gametophore development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCESA5 alone is sufficient for normal protonemal filament and leafy gametophore development in prior septuple knockout background. 2. Loss of PpCESA5 together with the other seven CESAs generates viable octuple mutants with normal protonemata but no gametophores. 3. Constitutive expression of PpCESA5 partially rescues gametophore development, showing CESA-dependent cellulose is specifically required for diffuse-growing gametophore tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane rosette cellulose synthesis complexes
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CESA proteins; CSI1 (discussed for CESA family generally)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of CESA activity causes abolished gametophore development, cell rupture, and early senescence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central experimentally, cellulose is a structural wall polymer rather than a classical nutritional metabolite; the paper contrasts CESA with CSLD-dependent cellulose synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like D (CSLD) family; glycosyltransferase with transmembrane helices, RING domain, plant-conserved region, class-specific region, catalytic D and QxxRW motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative cellulose microfibril synthesis in tip growth and cytokinesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes β-1,4-glucan that forms cellulose microfibrils, likely via rosette-shaped plasma membrane complexes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for cellulose deposition during tip growth/cytokinesis is implied, not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides structural support and cell wall formation during tip growth and cytokinesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall, especially protonemal tips and cell plates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In CESA-deficient P. patens, CSLDs are the most likely enzymes producing cellulose microfibrils and rosette CSC-like structures that sustain protonemal tip growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic removal of all CESAs; freeze-fracture TEM; x-ray diffraction; solid-state NMR; platinum shadowing of extracted walls; S4B cellulose staining; inference from prior CSLD localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All eight CSLD proteins localize to protonemal cell plates and cell tips; CSLD2 and CSLD6 are preferentially expressed in gametophores
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cell tips and adjacent to fusing cell plates in protonemal filaments
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tip growth and cytokinesis contexts; possible Ca2+ gradients and isoxaben insensitivity discussed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA-deficient protonemal filaments still synthesize cellulose microfibrils structurally indistinguishable from typical plant cellulose. 2. Plasma membranes of CESA-deficient filaments retain rosette particles concentrated at cell tips and cell plates, matching previous CSLD localization. 3. Therefore, proteins other than CESAs, most likely CSLDs, can form rosettes and produce cellulose microfibrils in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane at cell tips and cell plates
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely specialized for tip growth/cytokinesis rather than diffuse growth; may not substitute for CESAs in gametophore development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The assignment to CSLDs is strongly supported but indirect in this study; no CSLD knockout/complementation was performed here.
